timeline_html:
Summary. Nadine has been noticing progressive hair thinning for well over a year, with shedding still worsening despite some early signs of baby hairs returning. Her pattern suggests a long-standing hormonal influence rather than a short, acute shock.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • PCOS-related hormonal imbalance (ongoing; started before Mar 2025)
  • Low iron levels (resolved; date not provided)

Timeline forecast. Based on her hormonal and nutritional background, shedding likely intensified through mid‑2025 and began to stabilize toward early 2026. With iron repletion and improved nutrition, visible baby hairs in early 2026 fit the expected early regrowth phase. Full density recovery may continue through mid‑2026, though ongoing PCOS activity can slow this process. Dominant driver: PCOS-related hormonal imbalance—its chronic, ongoing nature explains the continued shedding and slower response, while iron deficiency likely amplified earlier loss.

Where you are now. You’re in the regrowth start phase—new hairs are emerging as shedding gradually eases. Keep supporting hormones and iron stores; steady routines now nurture stronger growth ahead. 🌱
